questions: Role: Expert Technical Interviewer.
Job Title: Full Stack.
Job Description: HTML, CSS (Note: Very limited JD, implies a need to expand to Full Stack concepts or focus heavily on the provided skills).
Interview Duration: 2 minutes (Extremely short - essentially a "speed round" or a "single-question focus").
Interview Type: Technical.
Goal: Generate engaging, relevant, natural, and conversational interview questions.
Constraints:
Plain text only.
One question per line.
No JSON, no brackets, no numbers, no special characters.
Cover: Technical, Behavioral, Problem Solving, Experience, Leadership.
Target Duration: 2 minutes (meaning the questions must be punchy and require concise answers).
Job Title says "Full Stack", but the JD only mentions "HTML CSS".
A "Full Stack" role usually implies Frontend, Backend, Databases, and APIs.

*Constraint Check:* 2 minutes is incredibly short. A typical interview involves 2-3 questions max. I should provide a variety of questions that *could* fit into a 2-minute window, ranging from technical deep-dives to behavioral snapshots.
*Category: Technical (HTML/CSS focus + Full Stack context)*
How do you approach making a website responsive and ensuring it works across all devices and screen sizes?
Can you explain the difference between Flexbox and CSS Grid and when you would choose one over the other?
How do you ensure your HTML structure is accessible to users with screen readers?
*Category: Behavioral (Teamwork/Learning)*
Describe a time you had a disagreement with a teammate over a technical decision; how did you resolve it?
When you're working on a complex feature and feel stuck, what's your process for getting back on track?
*Category: Problem Solving (Scenario-based)*
If a user reports that the layout looks broken on their mobile device but works perfectly on your desktop, what steps would you take to debug it?
Imagine you're tasked with optimizing a slow-loading webpage; where would you start looking for performance bottlenecks?
*Category: Experience/Projects*
What is a project you've worked on that you are most proud of, and what was your specific contribution to its success?
*Category: Leadership (Even for junior/mid-level, focus on ownership)*
How do you approach reviewing a teammate's code to ensure quality while maintaining a positive team dynamic?
Must be plain text.
One question per line.
No numbers/bullets.
No JSON.
Selection for the user:*
How do you ensure a web layout remains responsive and visually consistent across different screen sizes and devices?
Can you walk me through your process for debugging a CSS issue where an element isn't displaying where you expect it to?
If you were tasked with improving the accessibility of an existing web application, what are the first few things you would check?
Describe a time when you had to integrate a frontend component with a backend API; what challenges did you face during that process?
When you encounter a piece of code that is difficult to understand or poorly written, how do you approach refactoring it?
How do you balance the need for rapid feature development with the importance of writing clean, maintainable code?
Tell me about a time you had to explain a technical concept to a non-technical stakeholder or team member.
Full stack role? Yes (integration, refactoring, responsiveness).
HTML/CSS focus? Yes.
2-minute appropriate? Yes, these are high-impact, single-shot questions.
Plain text/no special characters/no numbers? Yes.
Self-Correction during drafting:* The user wants "questions" (plural). Even though the interview is 2 minutes, I will provide a set of options they can choose from.
Final List:*
How do you approach making a web layout responsive and ensuring it works seamlessly across all devices?
Can you explain a time you had to debug a complex CSS or layout issue and how you identified the root cause?
How do you ensure that the HTML you write follows modern accessibility standards for all users?
Tell me about a project where you had to connect a frontend interface to a backend service and what the biggest challenge was.
When you are working on a tight deadline, how do you decide which technical details to prioritize and which ones to simplify?
Describe a situation where you had to learn a new tool or framework very quickly to meet a project requirement.

If a teammate suggests a different technical approach than the one you proposed, how do you handle that conversation?How do you approach making a web layout responsive and ensuring it works seamlessly across all devices?
Can you explain a time you had to debug a complex CSS or layout issue and how you identified the root cause?
How do you ensure that the HTML you write follows modern accessibility standards for all users?
Tell me about a project where you had to connect a frontend interface to a backend service and what the biggest challenge was.
When you are working on a tight deadline, how do you decide which technical details to prioritize and which ones to simplify?
Describe a situation where you had to learn a new tool or framework very quickly to meet a project requirement.
If a teammate suggests a different technical approach than the one you proposed, how do you handle that conversation?
If a user reports that a website is loading very slowly, what is your step-by-step process for finding the bottleneck?
What is your preferred method for organizing your CSS to ensure it remains scalable and easy for other developers to read?
Tell me about a time you received feedback on your code that you didn't initially agree with; how did you respond?
